capabilities.title: Security Layer
capabilities.desc: Multi-level auth with dedicated middleware for input validation, error handling, and super-admin privilege separation.

capabilities.title: System Architecture
capabilities.desc: Four purpose-built systems with clear separation of concerns — each independently structured and deployable.

capabilities.title: Payment Lifecycle
capabilities.desc: Full Stripe integration covering checkout, webhooks, subscriptions, invoices, and refunds with the complete SDK wired end-to-end.

capabilities.title: Database Operations
capabilities.desc: Versioned database migrations with dedicated seed, rollback, and table-creation scripts — proper schema evolution over time.

capabilities.title: License Enforcement
capabilities.desc: End-to-end licensing from server-side key issuance to plugin-side feature gating and validation.

capabilities.title: Role-Based Access
capabilities.desc: Tiered admin system with separate super-admin, admin, and customer controllers — each with their own routes and UI.

meta.tagline: Four connected systems: WordPress plugin, Node.js license server, marketing site with Stripe + docs, and customer portal — clear API contracts and automated doc install.

overview.heading: What is MultiLang?
overview.body: MultiLang is a multilingual WordPress plugin sold as a subscription, with a full business stack around it. The plugin validates and activates licenses against a Node.js license server (ml). The marketing site runs Stripe Checkout; when payment is accepted and the customer is new, the marketing site creates the user on the license server (POST /api/auth/register) and provisions the license (POST /api/licenses). Existing customers get a new license only. The customer portal talks to the license server for auth, licenses, payments, and support tickets. The marketing theme also ships a documentation structure: custom post type, categories, and an automatic install that creates default pages, doc categories, and initial documentation content on theme activation.

systems.title: WordPress Plugin
systems.summary: The core product. Broad scope: 8 action handlers (clone, connect, translate, bulk copy, disconnect), admin & onboarding, full ACF support (flexible, repeater, group, options), licensing, menu system (core, customizer, duplication), permalinks, front page, review system, SEO, and settings for language, URL, menu, full-site translation, relationship mapping, strings, user roles, and source tracking. Admin and actions use AJAX throughout.
systems.detail: Actions layer (8 handlers): bulk_copy, change_language, clone_content, clone_page, connect, delete, disconnect_translation, translate_content. Includes: ACF (flexible content, repeater, group, options), licensing (feature gate, validator, manager), menu (core, customizer, display, duplication), permalinks (hook manager, handler, redirects), front page and page ordering, post states, review system, SEO, translation source tracking, language transition handler. Settings cover language, URL, menu, full-site translation, relationship mapping, review, source tracking, string translations (with auto-migrator and words-to-ignore), user roles, and dynamic variable declaration. Admin and settings UIs use AJAX throughout for license actions, translations, and settings updates. All user-facing strings are internationalized; supports bulk translation and Google Translate. 80+ flag assets, dedicated CSS/JS per surface.

systems.title: License Server
systems.summary: The backbone of the business logic. A Node.js REST API backed by one PostgreSQL cluster (3 databases); Helmet for security headers, Express rate limiting on endpoints; handles license validation, activation tracking, customer records, Stripe webhooks, and admin/ticketing.
systems.detail: Validates license keys on every plugin activation, tracks site activations against plan limits, stores customer and payment records, and serves as the single source of truth. Uses Helmet for security headers and Express rate limiting on API endpoints. Exposes an admin interface with dashboard, license generation, and customer management tools.

systems.title: Customer Portal
systems.summary: A self-service portal where customers manage licenses, view payment history, download invoices, request refunds, and update account settings.
systems.detail: All data is fetched in real time from the license server via authenticated API calls. Handles its own session management, nonce-based security, and input sanitization. Fully responsive with no framework dependency.

systems.title: Marketing Site
systems.summary: Public entry point for purchases plus built-in documentation. Stripe Checkout; on payment success the theme creates new customers and licenses on the license server via direct API calls. Theme includes a full doc structure with auto-install.
systems.detail: Creates Stripe Checkout sessions and redirects to Stripe. When checkout completes, the theme runs handle_checkout_session_completed: for new customers it calls the license server (POST /api/auth/register, then POST /api/licenses); for existing customers it only creates the license. So the marketing site does call the license API directly to create users and licenses after payment. The theme also provides a documentation system: custom post type (ml_docs), categories, and install.php that on theme activation creates default pages, doc categories, and initial documentation content.

cards.back: WordPress APIs (hooks, options, REST, menus), AJAX throughout for admin and actions (license, translate, settings). ACF integration for repeaters and flexible content, Google Translate API, full i18n with translatable strings.

Express.js, one PostgreSQL cluster (3 databases), Helmet for security headers, Express rate limiting, JWT auth, Stripe webhook handling (payment_intent, invoice, subscription events), admin UI, customer and ticket APIs, CORS.
WordPress theme, fetch-based authenticated REST consumption, server-side session and nonce management, vanilla JS, no CSS framework.

WordPress theme, Stripe PHP SDK (Checkout Sessions only; webhooks live on license server). Documentation: ml_docs CPT, doc_category taxonomy, install.php auto-creates pages, categories, and initial docs on theme activation. Vanilla JS, modular CSS, theme switcher.
API key authentication for inter-service calls, versioned database schema migrations, structured logging, environment-based config, Procfile for deployment.

items.title: End-to-end payment provisioning
items.summary: Connecting a Stripe payment event to a fully provisioned license across systems.
items.detail: Stripe sends webhooks to the license server (POST /webhooks/stripe); the marketing site only creates Checkout sessions and redirects. The license server validates the webhook, creates or updates customer and license records, and the portal is immediately usable. Reliable flow required webhook validation, idempotency, and clear API contracts so duplicate events never create duplicate licenses.

items.title: ACF deep field traversal
items.summary: Translating arbitrarily nested ACF field structures without corrupting metadata.
items.detail: ACF flexible content and repeater fields can be nested to any depth. The translation layer must correctly traverse the structure, identify which fields contain translatable strings, and write translated values back without touching layout keys, row indices, or field type metadata. A mistake at any level silently breaks the field layout for the customer.

items.title: License validation performance
items.summary: Validation must be fast and reliable since it runs on every plugin activation.
items.detail: In some configurations the license server is called on every WordPress page load. The API endpoint is deliberately lightweight — a single indexed query, no joins where avoidable, and a response structure that lets the plugin cache the result locally to avoid redundant calls.

items.title: Multi-system authentication
items.summary: Four systems with different auth requirements that must not share credentials inappropriately.
items.detail: The portal uses customer-facing session tokens scoped to the WordPress installation. The license server uses JWT for admin access. Inter-service communication between the marketing site, portal, and license server uses rotating API key headers. Each boundary was designed so that a compromised key in one layer could not escalate access in another.

items.title: Tiered Plans
items.desc: Free, Pro, and Agency plans with server-enforced limits on active sites and supported languages. Feature gating is handled inside the plugin via a dedicated licensing module.

items.title: Automated Licensing
items.desc: Purchase on the marketing site triggers a Stripe Checkout session. On payment confirmation, a webhook creates a license and customer record on the license server with no manual intervention required.

items.title: Customer Self-Service
items.desc: Customers manage everything independently — view and activate licenses, download invoices, request refunds, change passwords, and update notification preferences — without contacting support.

items.title: Admin Dashboard
items.desc: Internal admin interface on the license server for license management, customer lookup, usage statistics, API key management, and a ticketing system.

items.title: WordPress.org Ready
items.desc: The plugin is fully internationalized with all user-facing strings translatable, consistent text domain usage, and JavaScript localization via wp_localize_script. Passes WordPress.org submission requirements.

role.heading: Role
role.title: Solo Developer
role.body: Every part of this product was designed, built, and deployed by me. That includes architecture decisions across all four systems, all backend and frontend code, database schema design, Stripe integration, inter-service authentication, deployment configuration, and ongoing maintenance.
lines: PHP plugin development with WordPress APIs, AJAX throughout admin and actions
Node.js REST API with PostgreSQL, Helmet, Express rate limiting
